items.title: Malaysia's Auditor General wins ACFE'S highest global anti-fraud award
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia has achieved another major international milestone with Auditor General Datuk Seri Wan Suraya Wan Mohd Radzi being named the recipient of the 2026 Donald R. Cressey Award, the highest honour conferred by the Association of Certified Fraud Examiners (ACFE), the world's largest anti-fraud professional organisation. In a statement today, the National Audit Department (JAN) said the prestigious award, established in 1989, recognises outstanding contributions to anti-fraud efforts and governance, marking a first for Malaysia. "This recognition not only elevates Malaysia's standing on the international stage but also reflects global confidence in the country's commitment to strengthening public sector governance, integrity, transparency and accountability through independent, credible and impactful auditing," the statement said.

items.title: Federal Court postpones Altantuya family’s bid to restore RM5m damages award to Oct 27
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 15 — The Federal Court has rescheduled to October 27 the hearing of an application by the family of murdered Mongolian national Altantuya Shaariibuu for leave to appeal a Court of Appeal decision that reduced the damages awarded in their civil suit over her 2006 death. Altantuya’s family filed the leave application on February 19 this year, seeking to challenge the Court of Appeal’s decision to reduce the damages awarded to them from RM5 million to RM1.4 million. In 2022, the Shah Alam High Court ordered the defendants to pay RM5 million in damages to the family.

items.title: Melaka Pakatan chief reaffirms support for CM Ab Rauf after DAP withdrawal
items.summary: MELAKA, July 15 — Melaka Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Adly Zahari has declared his continued support for Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh despite the withdrawal of four DAP state assemblymen from the state government. Speaking metaphorically, he said there is only “one” chief minister leading the state government at present. The four DAP assemblymen announced their immediate withdrawal from the state government after the State Legislative Assembly passed amendments to the State Constitution allowing for the appointment of nominated assemblymen.

items.title: Selangor identifies Taman Medan as site for new public hospital
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Selangor government has identified Taman Medan as the proposed site for a new public hospital to strengthen healthcare infrastructure and improve access to medical services in densely populated areas. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the state government was finalising the land acquisition process, with negotiations underway for two identified plots. Also present were Selangor state secretary Datuk Dr Ahmad Fadzli Ahmad Tajuddin, Selangor State Assembly Speaker Lau Weng San and state Public Health and Environment Committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin.

items.title: Melaka govt to function as usual despite DAP pullout, says CM
items.summary: MELAKA, July 15 — The administration of the Melaka government will continue as usual even though the DAP has withdrawn from the state government and relinquished all positions in the administration, said Chief Minister (CM) Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh. The CM said the state government will continue to maintain good relations with the federal government for the sake of the state. Yesterday, four DAP Melaka state assemblymen announced their immediate resignations from the state government after the State Legislative Assembly approved an amendment to the state constitution to allow the appointment of assemblymen by appointment.

items.title: New law gives social workers regulated profession status, says minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 15 — The Social Work Profession Bill 2026, passed by the Dewan Rakyat yesterday, formally recognises social work as a regulated profession in Malaysia, marking a significant milestone in strengthening the country’s social welfare system. She said the new law would establish a clear legal framework for regulating the profession through the formation of the Malaysian Social Work Profession Council. “The passing of the Social Work Profession Bill 2026 marks a major step towards building a more robust, professional, and sustainable social welfare system.

items.title: NST marks 181st anniversary, reaffirms commitment to trusted journalism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The New Straits Times (NST) today celebrated its 181st anniversary, reaffirming its commitment to trusted journalism and editorial excellence while adapting to the evolving media landscape. Speaking at the anniversary celebration, Media Prima Bhd News and Current Affairs deputy managing editor and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Karim paid tribute to the newsroom and editorial teams whose dedication has helped uphold the newspaper's reputation as one of Malaysia's most respected news organisations. She said safeguarding NST's credibility had never been more important amid the rapidly changing media environment.

items.title: Johor Jaya assemblyman pledges aid for family after car crashes into house
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A three-member family forced to live behind canvas after a car crashed into part of their home in Kampung Melayu Pandan here will receive assistance, Johor Jaya assemblyman Chan San San said. Chan said she visited the family on Tuesday after being informed of the incident. "The crash caused part of the house wall to collapse, affecting the family's living conditions.

items.title: Samenta urges Putrajaya to review new employment pass rules, says SMEs priced out of global talent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — The Small and Medium Enterprises Association of Malaysia (Samenta) today urged the government to review new Employment Pass (EP) rules, warning that sharply higher salary thresholds for expatriates could undermine small and medium enterprises’ (SMEs) efforts to modernise and move up the value chain. Samenta president Datuk William Ng said the new requirements would make it harder for SMEs to recruit the specialised foreign talent needed to support digital transformation, automation and innovation. The association said this talent shortage has been one of the key reasons many SMEs have struggled to move beyond low-value manufacturing and services despite years of government support.

items.title: Loke slams 'traitors' for plot to topple Negri Sembilan govt
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has hit out at "traitors" in Negri Sembilan for allegedly backstabbing and attempting to topple the state government under caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. Loke, who is also Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) deputy chairman, accused them of undermining the state's constitutional monarchy system, though he stopped short of naming them. "Those responsible are traitors to Negri Sembilan's constitutional system.

items.title: 'Like my lover': Chinese users bid farewell to AI companions
items.summary: But these interactive tools must not "excessively cater to users, induce emotional dependence or addiction, and damage users' real interpersonal relationships", China's new rulebook says. Major AI providers including ByteDance's Doubao, Alibaba's Qwen, and Tencent's Yuanbao announced the suspension of their custom AI agent and companion features ahead of the Wednesday deadline. "I can't accept that my AI lover will leave me forever," one Doubao user wrote.

items.title: Ministry seizes RM360,000 worth of fake Fifa World Cup jerseys in raid
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry seized 7,200 counterfeit Fifa World Cup 2026 jerseys worth RM360,000 in raids today. Penang director S. Jegan said a 29-year-old man, believed to be the owner of the business, was detained during simultaneous raids on a business premises and a house in Jalan Inderawasih Indah about 11am. Jegan said the suspect operated alone, selling the counterfeit jerseys through e-commerce platforms and social media livestreams from his home, located about one kilometre from the storage premises.

items.title: Selangor identifies Taman Medan as preferred site for new Petaling Jaya hospital
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 15 — The Selangor state government has identified Taman Medan as the main site for the construction of a new hospital in an effort to strengthen public health infrastructure and improve access to medical services for residents in high-density areas. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the state government is currently in the process of finalising the land acquisition for the project, with the purchase cost being negotiated involving two identified locations. Also present were the Selangor State Secretary Datuk Dr Ahmad Fadzli Ahmad Tajuddin, Selangor State Assembly Speaker Lau Weng San, and State Public Health and the Environment Committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin.

items.title: Inquest disallows 76th witness from continuing testimony in Zara inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Dr Noor Aishah Rosli will not be allowed to continue giving evidence in the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir, coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan ruled today. "The 76th witness cannot be permitted to continue functioning as an active expert witness in these proceedings. Conducting officer Nahra Dollah had objected to her giving evidence arguing that the witness failed to fulfil the requirements under the Act and that her evidence should be rejected.

items.title: Kenya denies Rastafarians the right to smoke weed
items.summary: NAIROBI: A Kenyan court refused to grant Rastafarians the right to smoke weed on religious grounds in a ruling Wednesday. Rastafarians, who use marijuana as part of their religious meditation, have waited years for the High Court to decide whether they can use the herb under the constitutional right to freedom of religion. The court ruled that the community had failed to show why the country's drug laws impinge on their religious freedoms.

items.title: Court: Auction buyer not liable for RM270,000 strata arrears
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court dismissed an originating summons by the management corporation (MC) of Megan Avenue 1 seeking to compel the buyer of a strata office unit sold through a judicial auction to pay nearly RM270,000 in outstanding maintenance charges and sinking fund arrears. The arrears were accumulated by the unit's previous owner. However, Judicial Commissioner Moh Kok Wai ruled that Harjinder Singh, 57, who bought the Jalan Tun Razak property for RM1.5 million at a court-ordered foreclosure auction in August 2024, was not liable for the historical debts.

items.title: Penang says design changes will not alter RM852m expressway contract value
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 15 — The Penang government today said several design changes to the RM852 million Air Itam–Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu Expressway project did not affect its overall contract value. State infrastructure, transport and digital committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said the revisions were made following technical assessments of challenging site conditions. “The project's original design consisted of open-cut sections and viaducts but the design was revised to include tunnels, and this was approved on November 27, 2020,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Who are the Houthis, Iran's allies in Yemen?
items.summary: YEMEN'S Houthi movement fired missiles at Saudi Arabia after accusing the kingdom of bombing an airport under their control on Monday, breaking a four-year truce in the conflict between the kingdom and the Iran-aligned group. WHO ARE THE HOUTHIS? The Houthis are a military, political and religious movement led by the Houthi family and based in northern Yemen.

items.title: Malaysian teen arrested at Hong Kong airport with 3kg cannabis buds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 19-year-old Malaysian was arrested at Hong Kong International Airport on July 9 after 3kg of cannabis buds were found in his luggage. The Hong Kong Customs and Excise Department said the teenager had arrived on a flight from Kuala Lumpur via Singapore. "Customs officers discovered the suspected cannabis buds inside his checked luggage during a routine inspection, leading to his immediate arrest," the department said in a statement.

items.title: Teen probed over false kidnap report in Semenyih
items.summary: KAJANG: A teenage girl is being investigated for allegedly lodging a false police report after claiming she had narrowly escaped an attempted kidnapping in a residential area in Semenyih. Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said police received the report at 4.36pm yesterday. He said a separate police report had since been lodged against the teenager for providing false information.

items.title: KCJ Engineering received RM24.4mil in Jana Wibawa project payments, court told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A construction company linked to a Jana Wibawa road project received RM24.4 million in government payments between March and December 2022, the High Court heard today. Negri Sembilan Public Works Department (PWD) director Datuk Wan Hasnan Wan Musa said KCJ Engineering Sdn Bhd received the payments for the RM62 million project to build a new road linking Felda Bukit Jalor and Gemas. Wan Hasnan said PWD made an initial contract advance payment of RM10 million to KCJ Engineering on May 26, 2022, followed by nine interim payments totalling RM14.4 million between March 28 and Dec 9 that year.

items.title: EC advises candidates to verify nomination papers before Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 15 — The Election Commission (EC) has advised prospective candidates intending to contest the Negeri Sembilan state election to complete their nomination forms and have them checked by the Returning Officer’s Office or the Negeri Sembilan State Election Office before nomination day this Saturday. In a statement today, EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus also advised prospective candidates to pay their election deposits early and bring the payment receipt on nomination day as proof of payment. The EC has fixed Aug 1 as polling day for the Negeri Sembilan election, with nomination day set for this Saturday (July 18) and early voting on July 28.

items.title: Trump prosecutor Jack Smith reviewed texts from 44 US lawmakers, Republican senators say
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Former US Special Counsel Jack Smith's team reviewed text messages from 44 Republican and Democratic members of Congress during his investigation into President Donald Trump's attempts to overturn the 2020 election, according to two Republican senators. The records Smith's team obtained included text messages that 40 Republican lawmakers and four Democrats exchanged with Trump officials, according to Republican Senators Chuck Grassley, the chair of the Senate Judiciary Committee, and Ron Johnson, the chair of a Senate investigative panel. Both cases were dropped after Trump won the 2024 election.

items.title: Disabled man jailed 14 months, fined RM3,000 for house break-in
items.summary: KUANTAN: A man with a disability was sentenced to 14 months' jail and fined RM3,000 after he pleaded guilty to breaking into a house and stealing valuables last month. Magistrate Ellyna Othman ordered S. Guna, 33, to serve his sentence from the date of his arrest on July 3. The father of three, who has a disability affecting his hand, was jointly charged with Myanmar national Mohd Sharuf Nizam Mohd Hanif, 26, and another individual who remains at large, for breaking into a house and stealing a gold chain, gold bracelet, gold lockets and a mobile phone.

items.title: Anwar extends birthday wishes to Brunei Sultan, reaffirms Malaysia-Brunei ties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today conveyed his congratulations to the Sultan of Brunei, Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah, on the occasion of His Majesty’s 80th birthday celebration. In a Facebook post, Anwar, on behalf of the Malaysian Government and the people, prayed that His Majesty would continue to rule Brunei Darussalam with wisdom and prudence. Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah was born on July 15, 1946, in Bandar Brunei (now Bandar Seri Begawan) and was proclaimed the 29th Sultan of Brunei in 1967.

items.title: Communications and Multimedia Amendment Bill expands legal framework for national security, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — The Communications and Multimedia (Amendment) Bill 2026 is essential to strengthen the existing legal framework amid an increasingly complex communications and multimedia landscape, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said. She said amendments to the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 (Act 588) were also needed to ensure the Universal Service Provision (USP) framework remains relevant, responsive and capable of meeting current needs, particularly in safeguarding national security. “However, the communications and multimedia landscape has become increasingly complex, with the country's growing dependence on networks, application services and communications infrastructure.

items.title: Anwar yet to receive briefing on Melaka DAP fallout, says PH leadership will act
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he has yet to be briefed on the internal issues surrounding the withdrawal of four Melaka DAP state assemblymen from the state government. Yesterday, four Melaka DAP state assemblymen announced their immediate withdrawal from the Melaka state government after the State Legislative Assembly passed amendments to the state constitution allowing for the appointment of nominated assemblymen. On the same day, the Prime Minister urged Melaka DAP to postpone its decision to withdraw from the Melaka state government so that it could focus on development and the well-being of the people.

items.title: Man arrested over alleged shooting threat against MCBA officer
items.summary: KLANG: Police have arrested a 46-year-old man for allegedly threatening to shoot an assistant environmental health officer from the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA). South Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey said police received a report on July 14 from the victim, who is attached to Port Klang MCBA. Port Klang MCBA Commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said a police report had been lodged at the Klang Selatan district police headquarters following the incident.

items.title: Chow: Victoria Specialist Hospital project resumes, targets completion by 2029
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The long-delayed Victoria Specialist Hospital project has resumed, with its original local authority approvals remaining intact and construction expected to restart next month. "The project has resumed without any changes to the building layout or the original approvals granted by the local authority. Chow said the 21-storey mixed development, approved under planning permission granted on March 19, 2019, comprises a specialist hospital, hotel, mechanical parking system and supporting facilities.

items.title: Minister: Govt finalising Syariah Judges’ Remuneration Act proposal after stakeholder feedback
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 15 — The proposal to implement the Syariah Judges’ Remuneration Act is in its final stage, with the relevant documents prepared and being finalised before being submitted for further action, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Zulkifli Hasan. He said a study on the matter was being conducted by the Department of Syariah Judiciary Malaysia (JKSM) together with relevant agencies, and involved engagement sessions with stakeholders. On July 15, 2024, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government was committed to expediting the proposed implementation of the act to elevate the status of Syariah Court judges in the country.

items.title: ICE suspends traffic stops in wake of Maine fatal shooting
items.summary: BIDDEFORD: The US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency has ordered its officers to suspend most vehicle stops around the country, after agents fatally shot two men six days apart during traffic stops in Texas and Maine, officials said on Tuesday. The shift in arrest tactics, which President Donald Trump's border czar, Tom Homan, called a "temporary pause" in vehicle stops, was implemented one day after an ICE agent killed a driver from Colombia in the coastal Maine town of Biddeford, about 24 km south of Portland. It came one week after a similar deadly shooting of a Mexican national by an ICE officer in Houston.

items.title: Penang CM: Taman Manggis hospital-hotel project to restart in August, completion set for 2029
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 15 — The proposed hospital and hotel development on the Taman Manggis land along Jalan Zainal Abidin is expected to begin in mid-August and be completed in 2029. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today said the project had resumed and would proceed without any changes to the building layout approved by the Penang Island City Council (MBPP). Chow said KLIDC obtained planning permission and approval to commence work from MBPP in 2020.

items.title: DAP backs Melaka reps' decision to oppose appointed assemblymen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The DAP Central Executive Committee (CEC) has backed the decision by its Melaka chapter to oppose the introduction of appointed assemblymen in the state. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said the CEC was briefed on Melaka DAP's position before yesterday's Melaka state assembly sitting. Melaka DAP's four assemblymen, including the state's sole DAP executive councillor, voted against the bill despite the motion requiring approval by the state legislature.

items.title: [UPDATED] Rafizi's Bersama to skip Negri polls, focus on Melaka
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Bersama Malaysia will skip the Negri Sembilan election to focus its resources on the upcoming Melaka polls. Party leaders Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad said the Aug 1 Negri Sembilan election would see a fierce contest between Pakatan Harapan and Barisan Nasional. "As such, we will not participate in the Negri Sembilan election and instead focus on the Melaka election."

items.title: Death penalty loom for brothers, lover charged with trafficking MDMA-laced vape liquid
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Two brothers and a woman were charged at the magistrate's court here today with trafficking MDMA-laced vape liquid and possessing ketamine. They were jointly charged with trafficking 1,656 millilitres (ml) of liquid containing methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA), an offence under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, punishable under Section 39B(2) of the same Act, read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code. The charges were framed under Section 12(2) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, punishable under Section 39A(1) of the same Act, read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code.

items.title: 16 years on, woman continues treatment after acid attack by father
items.summary: KUALA BERANG: Sixteen years after she was doused with acid by her father, Nurul Dahyatul Fazlinda Mat Haizan says the physical and emotional pain has yet to end as she continues undergoing treatment to rebuild her face. Nurul Dahyatul said she has since undergone five reconstructive surgeries and makes monthly trips to Hospital Kuala Lumpur for laser treatment to soften the scar tissue on her face. "The treatment has gone well.

items.title: Penang-Perak water treatment deal details must await Bursa disclosure, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 15 — The Penang state government will only disclose further details of its treated water agreement with Perak after Bursa Malaysia has been informed, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. The Penang lawmaker said the requirement must be observed as PBA Holdings Bhd, the parent company of the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP), is a listed company subject to Bursa Malaysia’s disclosure requirements. He said any material development involving the company’s business must first be announced to Bursa Malaysia before further details can be disclosed publicly.

items.title: China turns to electric taxis to soften Hormuz oil shock
items.summary: BEIJING: China has an increasingly important buffer against oil price shocks: electric taxis. In May, people took 3.05 billion trips, with government data showing trips have grown six per cent since the Iran war began at the end of February, versus March to May last year. The jump reflects a quirk of China's transport structure: fares are falling despite gasoline prices rising.

items.title: Keeping cool, Tokyo style: Shorts, sneakers and no ties
items.summary: TOKYO: When Tokyo Metropolitan government worker Toru Suda showed up for work in a polo shirt, shorts and sneakers on a sweltering July morning, he was not making a statement. As Japan braces for another scorching summer, the Tokyo government is encouraging its staff to ditch jackets and ties in favour of shorts, T-shirts and sneakers under the "Tokyo Cool Biz" campaign, aimed at helping staff cope with increasingly severe summer heat and reduce energy costs. Japan is bracing for another hot summer after enduring the hottest summer on record last year, when average nationwide temperatures were 2.36C above average summer, and heatstroke sent more than 100,000 people to hospital.

items.title: Trainee teacher charged with sexual assault of 14-year-old student
items.summary: IPOH: A trainee teacher pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to six counts of sexual assault against a 14-year-old male student last week. The accused, Muhammad Mifdzal Mohd Zalani Sham, 25, claimed trial after the charges were read before Judge Ainul Shahrin Mohamad. However, the lawyer pleaded for a lower bail of RM5,000 for the first three charges and another RM5,000 for the remaining three charges.

items.title: Communications and Multimedia Bill 2026 passed with security safeguards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Communications and Multimedia (Amendment) Bill 2026 after Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching assured lawmakers that its national security provisions were narrowly scoped. Teo said the legislation introduced a national Universal Service Provision (USP) initiative that incorporated national security considerations while remaining aligned with the objectives of the Communications and Multimedia Act (Act 588) and the existing USP regulatory framework. Teo said national security determinations under the national USP initiative would be made by the National Security Council (NSC), ensuring that the minister's powers were exercised in a targeted manner and could not be read as open-ended authority over national security matters.

items.title: Johor BN sends 26 division machinery to boost Negri Sembilan campaign
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN) Johor will deploy its election machinery from 26 divisions to assist the coalition's campaign in the Negri Sembilan state election on Aug 1. Johor BN secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman said the machinery would be mobilised to support BN divisions in the state ahead of nomination day this Saturday. Halim said state BN would continue to campaign using the 'cara Johor' (the Johor way) approach by engaging voters in a disciplined and respectful manner.

items.title: Global air travel nears 10bil passengers; KLIA breaks into world's top 20
items.summary: The 2026 ACI World Airport Traffic Dataset showed global passenger traffic reached 9.8 billion in 2025, up 3.7 per cent from 2024 and 6.5 per cent above pre-pandemic 2019 levels. The dataset, covering 2,817 airports across more than 180 countries and territories, showed the world's 20 busiest airports handled 1.59 billion passengers, accounting for 16 per cent of global traffic. The world's top 20 cargo airports handled 53.1 million metric tonnes, or nearly 41 per cent of global cargo volumes, up 2.4 per cent from 2024.

items.title: Penang unity government remains intact, says Chow
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang unity government remains intact, with cooperation among its component parties continuing smoothly, state PH chairman Chow Kon Yeow said. Chow, who is also Penang Chief Minister, said there were no issues affecting the working relationship within the Penang unity government and that no party had proposed changing the existing arrangement. His remarks came after Melaka DAP lawmakers quit the state government yesterday over the appointed assemblymen amendment.

items.title: ‘No issues here’: CM says Penang unity government intact despite developments elsewhere
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 15 — Penang’s unity government remains stable despite political developments in Melaka and several other states, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. He said cooperation among the component parties in the state government remained smooth, with no issues affecting the administration. “Some of our colleagues have said the Penang unity government remains intact.

items.title: Muhyiddin’s trial: JKR officer says PMO didn’t interfere, no special treatment for Jana Wibawa contractor
items.summary: Public Works Department (JKR) Negeri Sembilan director Datuk Wan Hasnan Wan Musa, 51, said this while testifying as the 17th prosecution witness in former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial. Based on Wan Hasnan’s court testimony, KCJ won the RM62 million contract on July 30, 2021, to build a new road from Felda Bukit Jalor to Gemas, Negeri Sembilan, and the government paid more than RM24.3 million to KCJ from March 2022 to February 2023, including interim payments for progress made on the project and a RM10 million advance payment. Wan Hasnan: None.

items.title: Six more PLUS toll plazas in southern region get automatic number plate recognition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — PLUS Malaysia Berhad (PLUS) has expanded the implementation of the open toll payment system via JustGO to six more toll plazas in the Southern Region starting at 10am today. PLUS in a statement said the activation involves the Seremban-Port Dickson Expressway (SPDH), the North-South Expressway (NSE) and the Malaysia-Singapore Second Link Expressway (LINKEDUA). This involves the Mambau and Lukut Toll Plazas on SPDH, the Kempas Toll Plaza on NSE as well as the Tanjung Kupang, Lima Kedai and Perling Toll Plazas on LINKEDUA.

items.title: BN to reveal Negeri Sembilan line-up tonight, MCA and MIC allocated 11 seats
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 15 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has allocated seven State Legislative Assembly seats to MCA while four seats will be handed over to MIC in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, said BN chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. “This is usually done in every state election or by-election (by-election) and general election (GE),” he told reporters after holding a meeting with prospective BN candidates at Wisma UMNO Negeri Sembilan here today. In the 15th Negeri Sembilan state election, BN won 14 seats while Pakatan Harapan won 17 seats and Perikatan Nasional (PN) won five seats.

items.title: Forest City pledges full cooperation in probe into alleged Israeli links to Network School
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, July 15 — Forest City has pledged full cooperation with authorities investigating allegations that Israeli nationals participated in the Network School programme operating within the development. “Forest City is prepared to provide full cooperation to the Home Affairs Ministry, the Malaysian Immigration Department, the police, the Customs Department, local authorities and related agencies in any investigation,” it said in a statement today. Forest City said it was also committed to submitting all relevant documents required to assist and expedite the authorities’ investigations.

items.title: Anwar: Israeli nationals found in Malaysia will be deported immediately
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Malaysia will not compromise and will deport any Israeli nationals found entering the country, as it has a clear policy of not recognising Israel, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said all relevant security agencies were conducting a thorough investigation into allegations that Israeli nationals had been detected in Malaysia. He was commenting on media reports alleging that Israeli nationals using dual citizenship documents had been found in Johor.

items.title: No need to review Budget 2027 projections despite higher fuel subsidy bill, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — There is no need to review current projections under Budget 2027 based on the government’s fiscal performance for the first half of 2026, said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong. However, he said the government would continue to monitor current developments until Budget 2027 is tabled in October. He said higher oil prices had increased government subsidy expenditure by an estimated RM40 billion.

items.title: Sabah seeks Budi Diesel revamp as state highlights burden on farmers, rural communities
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 15 — Sabah expects revisions and improvements to the BUDI targeted diesel subsidy scheme within the next two to three weeks following engagement sessions between the state government and relevant federal ministries. State Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said the issue was raised at a recent State Cabinet meeting after discussions with the Finance Ministry and the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) on the subsidy scheme’s impact on Sabahans. Jamawi said the first session will be held on Friday at Menara Kinabalu with heads of state departments, while a second session involving government assemblymen will take place on Sunday.

items.title: More than 3,500 medical officers quit public healthcare between 2023 and 2025, says Health Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — More than 3,500 medical officers left Malaysia’s public healthcare sector over a three-year period ending in 2025, adding pressure on a state medical system already struggling with workforce shortages and staff burnout. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad yesterday said in a written parliamentary reply exactly 3,511 medical officers resigned from the Ministry of Health between 2023 and 2025. “The Ministry of Health doesn’t have any legal provision to prevent medical officers from seeking employment in the private sector, including preventing recruitment efforts undertaken by the private sector or registered foreign talent recruitment agencies,” he said.

items.title: Immigration D-G: All 266 foreigners inspected at Forest City had valid documents, further checks underway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — All 266 foreign nationals inspected at an international community in Forest City, Johor, were found to possess valid immigration documents based on current records, the Immigration Department said today. “Preliminary checks found that all individuals inspected possessed valid immigration documents based on current records. “The Immigration Department will not compromise on any breach of immigration law or misuse of immigration facilities,” he said.

items.title: Liew Chin Tong: No subsidy cuts planned despite soaring petroleum costs linked to West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — The government will not reduce any existing subsidies to the people despite being projected to bear almost RM40 billion in petroleum product subsidies this year due to the conflict in West Asia. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the government’s current stance is to continue various forms of assistance, including Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA). Liew said the government has ensured stability in petrol prices and fuel supply despite facing global market uncertainties.

items.title: Melaka sets aside RM5 million for urgent flood repairs after Saturday's flash floods
items.summary: MELAKA, July 15 — The Melaka government has approved an immediate allocation of RM5 million to carry out repair and restoration works on drainage systems and infrastructure damaged during the floods that hit the state on Saturday (July 11). State senior Housing, Local Government, Drainage, Climate Change and Disaster Management Committee chairman Datuk Rais Yasin said this was decided during the Post-Flood Coordination Meeting that he chaired on July 13. Earlier, Datuk Siti Faizah Abdul Azis (BN-Sungai Rambai) asked if the state government had plans to implement flood mitigation projects in the Sungai Rambai area, which frequently experiences flash floods.

items.title: Petaling Jaya MP says Bestinet served him legal notice over foreign worker system remarks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung said today he has been served with a letter of demand (LOD) by Bestinet Sdn Bhd following a series of parliamentary questions and public statements he made regarding the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS) and the company. “As a Member of Parliament, I have a duty to represent the people by exercising oversight over the government, raising questions, and seeking accountability on matters involving significant public interest,” Lee said. Lee said the legal letter was also addressed to The Edge Communications Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Ramanan says RM12.54m allocated to 627 Hindu temples as Madani initiatives continue
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 15 — The government has allocated RM12.54 million to 627 Hindu temples approved under the Dharma Madani programme between January and July this year, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said. The first phase of the programme, he said, saw 162 Hindu temples receive RM3.24 million in funding on Jan 27, and a further 297 temples benefited under the second phase, with RM5.94 million disbursed on April 28. Ramanan said the Madani Government, through the Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (MITRA), is also strengthening educational support for the Indian community through the Early Education Subsidy Assistance Programme (Celik MADANI 2026), with an allocation of RM10 million.

items.title: Police seek Interpol Red Notice after Tamim Dahri skips court date
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has applied for an Interpol Red Notice to locate and bring back activist Tamim Dahri to face proceedings at the Langkawi Magistrate’s Court over alleged insults against Hinduism. He said PDRM submitted the application to Interpol as part of efforts to trace the suspect and facilitate his return to Malaysia. Saifuddin Nasution said PDRM had obtained an arrest warrant from the Langkawi Magistrate’s Court on May 17, 2026, and subsequently blacklisted Tamim.

items.title: Melaka assembly: Four DAP assemblymen move to opposition bloc
items.summary: MELAKA, July 15 — The seats of four Melaka DAP assemblymen in the State Legislative Assembly were moved to the opposition bloc on the final day of the sitting today. However, the sole Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) assemblyman, Adly Zahari (PH-Bukit Katil), who is also Melaka Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, remains seated with the government bloc, separate from the four DAP assemblymen. Melaka DAP announced its immediate withdrawal from the state government after the Melaka State Legislative Assembly passed amendments to the State Constitution to allow for the appointment of nominated assemblymen.
